data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats self-consistency only when the SC majority is non-unanimous but correct AND the critic achieves precision > 1 - (margin - 1)/(2*margin) at recall ~1, where margin is the SC vote gap; decisive predictor is the corrected-margin yield, not raw precision.", "mechanism": "Let the SC majority have margin m = floor(k/2)+1 (bare majority) up to k (unanimous). SC is already correct whenever m > k/2. Harness changes the outcome ONLY if the critic removes enough wrong minority candidates to flip a wrong majority (gain) OR removes enough right majority candidates to flip a correct majority (loss). Net gain requires critic recall on wrong candidates times the number of wrong minority candidates to exceed critic false-positive rate on right candidates times the majority buffer (m - k/2). For a bare majority (m = (k+1)/2, buffer = 1), a single false flag on a right candidate can flip the answer, so precision must be near 1.0; for larger margins the precision bar relaxes. The decisive quantity is therefore expected corrected-margin yield: (recall * minority_wrong_count) - (FPR * majority_buffer) > 0, evaluated per question.", "scope": {"covers": ["odd k from 5 to 21", "per-question base error e in (1/(k+1), k/(k+2))", "critic precision and recall that vary with question difficulty", "non-unanimous SC majorities where margin > 1", "base errors high enough that some SC majorities are wrong but low enough that some are right"], "excludes": ["unanimous SC pools (filtering cannot change outcome)", "base error so high that SC majority is almost always wrong (critic would need near-perfect recall to recover)", "k < 5 (majority too coarse for margin analysis)", "critic precision fixed independent of question"], "forbidden": [{"case": "Experiment yields harness accuracy strictly greater than SC accuracy at k=5 with per-question base error 0.04-0.08 and critic precision < 0.50 at recall > 0.80", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment yields net fixed-minus-broke > 0 in the regime where >70% of SC majorities are unanimous", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment shows harness accuracy below SC when critic FPR on right-majority candidates exceeds critic recall on wrong-minority candidates", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "Supersedes 5b4538abe4a7 by rejecting fixed-precision analysis: the same critic precision can help or hurt depending on the SC margin distribution. The v4-pro hard-set null result (net 0, 4/5 majorities already right, recall 1.0, precision 0.125) is explained: with margin 4 and buffer 2, FPR 0.875 on right candidates destroys more than recall 1.0 on the single wrong candidate can fix. The v4-flash easy-set null is explained by ceiling effects. Decisive experiment targets the mid-zone where SC majorities are bare (margin = 3 at k=5) and correct, making them fragile to false flags but also flippable when wrong."}
